ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Asɛ ɔmee lawɔ o ŋɔ, “Bonɔ ɔma kɛrɛdɛ! Bonɔ ɛɛ ɛ Korowii demii. Bonɔ ɛ doonyoa baa kpɔ o ta dɛɛ kaa nyundua.”
translated: Then he said to them, "Indeed, listen! Indeed, you are Korowii's child. Indeed, you will not see your father's face again.

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Asɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ ba be ŋɔ, “Wee ta nyɛɡre he ba boa peera nɔ nyoa la, hé tɛa nee a nyɔ dɛ berɛ kpɔa kaa dibɔr.”
translated: Jacob's son said to him, "If you don't go and do what I tell you, you will never eat anything good again.

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Belaa ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won ɛ ɡɛ, nemenɛ a bwa ba boa nyoa bonɔ, ásɛ ya ɡbili bwe nɔ le kɔlɔŋ nɔ nyoa mɔɔ dɛ́ tɛa nee a nyɔ.”
translated: Belaa said to them, "You know that when a person gives birth to a child, they will take care of it with love and care until it grows up.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Tɔpere la aa tela la, Jekɔb ŋɔ Laban be ŋɔ, “Bene anuanɛ la telo, tɛ mɛ n haah la ásɛ n mo name.”
translated: As they were going, Jacob said to Laban, "If you return, you will not see my daughter again.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Kɛ a jwaana la, Leaa Laban berɛ lawɛ ba tɛ Jekɔb asɛ o ne o ka chɔ.
translated: When they were done, Leah and Laban gave their children to Jacob so that he would marry them.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Asɛ o kpoŋe tea dɛ lolɛ bibaa tɛ Jekɔb.
translated: And he gave tea to Jacob's mother.

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Asɛ Lea ŋɔ, “Lɛɛ haa lɛ n jaa nɔ ɡbo ta maɡra aa, he berɔ dɛɛ nyin he lɛ n bie kwaa nɔ o be ja aa?” Anee Rakɛl ŋɔ, “Dɛ hɛɛ tɛ mɛ he bie kwaa nɔ anee yaa, mɛɛ sɛoh dɛ tɛ he ne Jekɔb kalaŋre jwaana nɔ ne.”
translated: And Leah said, "If you give me some mandrakes, then will he sleep with you tonight?" And Rachel said, "Therefore he will sleep with you tonight, because of Jacob's mand

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Anomanta, o lolɛ bibaa dɛ ŋɔ, “Korowii tɛ mɛ bibaa dɛ laa sihiih kpɔ n sie.”
translated: Anomanta told his child, “Korowii, don't let your child go to the farm to check my traps.

ref: But Laban told him, “If you really are my friend, stay on, and I'll pay whatever you ask. I'm sure the Lord has blessed me because of you.”
text: Gel n jemɛ kembɔr maa chɛ kɛ n kpɔ tɛɛ, ásɛ n nyin tɛɛ.”
translated: Gel and his wife were going to fetch water and drink water.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekɔb pere a bii la le anɔ la ta, dɛ ɡel anɔ la aa kaa la sie chaɡɛ Laban kwaa la waa paaɡe daa a sie la ne ambiŋere la be. Asɛ dɛ teɡɛ o ɡbabir kwaa la kaa baŋ bɛl, o ta an kpɔe koo Laban kwaa la ta.
translated: Jacob knew his daughter was pregnant, and that the child she was carrying belonged to Laban's family, and that she would give birth to it among her relatives. But if her husband's family

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: Peera la teene kpoŋa saŋ la, n deero dɛ na kɛŋɔ bombala aa dɛ jweera la peeɡɔ dɛ lɛɡre.
translated: When it is an important occasion, you should wear the bombala and tie the headtie properly.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb ta ɡelɛ Laban jemɛ kɛŋɔ o dɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: Jacob went to his home with Laban's sheep.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Laban la Jekɔb be dɛ ka ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he ɛɛ mɛ ɡɛ nɔɔ? He neɡ mɛ dɛ kpɔ n bihaahna chwa kɛŋɔ haa kaa yo laa dɛ laɡ ba la.
translated: Laban and Jacob spoke to each other, saying, "Why have you done this? You have stolen my daughters' hearts and carried them off like captives of the sword.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Dɛ berɛ ŋɔ, “Dɛ he ka dɛ fiiɡi n bihaahna nɔ kɛɛ dɛ ka mo haahna ja ba nɛ aa, a lole kɛŋɔ maa belaa do te, kɛ Korowii te ɛɛ nao.
translated: He said, "If he is a fig tree and has fruit, but if he has no fruit at all, you will know that it is a good thing, and it is not Kor

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Tɔ aa pera chɔmbibi la, Laban mo o bihaahna ne o naanar la lwɛrɛme, dɛ swee andɛɛɡa tɛɛ ba dɛ berɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: So when they had finished eating, Laban blessed him and his daughters and sent them away, and they went on their way and returned to their home.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: And Jacob built a pillar and named the place where he met Esau. And Esau became a great nation, and that place was called Edom.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Kondɛɛra waa peɡri a bini la, o ŋɔ bá kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ, a bɛlbwa kapa ne nɛn waa dɛ nyiŋa. Asɛ dɛ ŋɔ o bonɔɔna la be ŋɔ, “Hé tɔ n sie, ásɛ dɛ ɡel he baahna tɛr.”
translated: Kondɛɛra was walking in the bush, he saw something on the ground, a dead small animal that was pregnant. Then he called his friend and said, “Hey, come and see what has happened here.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb chiɡ ŋwɛnɛ ta dɛɛ kaa, asɛ nyundua toroma ba chemo.
translated: Jacob went to the farm and found a sheep with its lamb.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: O liiro kɛŋɔ dɛ Esɔ ba dɛ yo baŋ bɛl nɛra aa, ba kar la ɛɛ won dɔ bɔr dɛ́ chwa kaa.
translated: The story of Esau, when he was still a young man, he did not know how to live well.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chɔ bela awola jwaana la bwa, asɛ dɛ chale o kondɛɛra la bini ane dɛ́ kpɔ ka tɛ Esɔ.
translated: Jacob had a very bad dream because he was afraid he would encounter evil and see his brother Esau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Hé berɛ ɡel ó jemɛ kɛŋɔ o bonɔɔ Jekɔb ɡe lɔ habɔɔ dɛɛ ba. Dɛ ń tɔ sie, dɛ́ kpɔ koŋkɛɛla nɔ ba tɛɛ, ásɛ mɔɔ o tel he be. Ásɛ dɛ he nao waa, he ken ɛɛ kpɔɔ kɛɔ.”
translated: When the time came for Jacob's sons to be born, they were born there. And it happened that they saw a very beautiful woman there. And they said, "We will take

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to Esau his brother. And Esau lived in the land which is called Edom.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Jekɔb aa no ɡɛ la, o ɔmɔ kohona o bwara yɔr, asɛ dɛ bɔne o nɛra la ne peera ne nae, dɛ nyoɡmare la bini bɔre anɛ.
translated: When Jacob saw it, he became very angry because of the evil the men had done and the disgrace that had come upon them.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: he chaŋe ŋɔ, ‘He bonɔɔ Jekɔb kwaa. O lawa ŋɔ ń kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ Esɔ. O ɡe lɔ mun ta dɛɛ ba.’ ”
translated: He said, 'He will meet Jacob there. He will go and see what he will do to Esau. He will not say anything to them.

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Dɛ Jekɔb noɔ kɛŋɔ Sɛkem ɡuɡri o bihaah Daena o ne o chɔ, kɛ bela la, o ta wobɛlbwa chaŋɛ, dɛkalkɛ dɛ o bibaala la kaa nae ne dibɔr ba ta balɛ mɔɔ.
translated: Jacob went to the city of Shechem with his daughter Dinah and his children. When they arrived there, he did not stay long, because his relatives were not there to help him.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: dɛ Jekɔb bii la ɡe denɔ le kpɔɡ bini ba. Baa baa no waa la la, a we ba nɛ, asɛ ba baah di kohona kɛŋɔ Sɛkem aa ɛ wedoŋa dɛ nyiŋi Israel tena nyubaah kɛŋɔ waa ɡuɡri ba nimambie o ne o chɔ la.
translated: Jacob's sons were shepherds with their father's flock. While they were there, they were told that they had gone to graze in Shechem, and that the people of Israel were

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Gel ya nyoa pɛ yaa mo he bihaahna, ásɛ he ɡe ɛɛ mo ya kwa.
translated: God is good, He has no equal, and there is no one like Him.

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Anee ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won so nyiŋi ba ɛ ya nimambie kɛŋɔ hanjwamane.”
translated: Anee replied, "You will not be able to teach your children well.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Jekɔb ne o bii aa dɛ ba bela ira la, Korowii ɡelɔ ɔmee kohona chole nɛra baa lɔ bwɛɛre aa bɛne bela tena bwa nɛ ba ta wone ti ba dɛ ɛɛ ba wobɛlbwa.
translated: When Jacob and his children were in trouble, God sent a wise man to them to help them, and he showed them the way of life so that they would not die and perish.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Waa kaa so harɛ la ta la, Rubin kaa lawɛ tolo baa dɛ yerɔ Bilha baa lawa tɛ o mɛɛ o ɛ o sɛno la chɔ, asɛ Jekɔb no. Bii fi dɛ banɛɛ Jekɔb lolɛ.
translated: When he finished that, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah, his father's wife and concubine. Jacob heard about it. And the sons of Jacob were twelve.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Dɛkalkɛ dɛ harɛ o ne Jekɔb aa soɡe o ta la aa kanɛ tɛɛ ba. Ba dɔ kondɛɛra asɛ a ya, anomanta, dɛ a chɛ kɛ bɛlbwa nyin bofale dɛ́ ka so o kwaa ne.
translated: The next day the chief and Jacob went to the farm to cut grass. They worked very hard until the sun was hot and they were very thirsty.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Sɛɛ waa ɛ Hɔr te bibaala waa lol ba asɛ ba so Edom laanee, Lotan ne Sobal ne Sibeon dɛ Ana,
translated: These are the sons of Hor who lived in the land of Edom: Lotan and Sobal and Sibeon and Ana.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Asɛ Esa ɡe bibaalaa, Bilhan ne Saavan dɛ Akan.
translated: Asɛ Esa's children are Bilhan and Saavan, and they are Akan.

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Kooro Baal Hanan ɡe aa baa sewɛ la, Hadad chiɡ o naa ta, dɛ di kora. Dɛ o bwɛjen sɔne Pau. O haah ɡe sɔne Mɛhetabɛl, o ɛ Matrɛd bihaah. Matrɛd ɡe nya sɔne Mɛsahab.
translated: Koro Baal Hanan died, and Hadad succeeded him as king. And his wife was Pau. His mother was Mehetabel, and she was the daughter of Matred. Matred was the daughter of Mesahab.

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Awola bɛl, o dɔɔranar la kpɔ o mɛɛ kondɛɛra la kaa dibɔr Sɛkem pera,
translated: Awola's house, he built it and found it, he finished it and went to live in Shechem.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Dɛ berɛ ŋɔ, “He bɛ on kpo. Hé kpɔɔ ta kɔlɔŋ nɔ bɛl bini.” Gɛ aa ɛ o won lɛo le ba nɔn ta, dɛ́ kpɔɔ ka tɛ ba mɛɛ la waa o bol ɡɛ.
translated: Then he said, "Come and see." He saw a big tree and a small bird. But when he went to look at the tree, he saw that it was not a tree but a person.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Asɛ o berɛ la Juda be dɛ ka ŋɔ, “Maa laŋa la, n ta on nae, asɛ dɛ bɔɔ baala bane baa lɔ bela o wa, ba ɡe ŋɔ hanjwamane bɛlbwa toole bela.”
translated: At that time, the Jews said to him, “If you are the Christ, tell us plainly, so that we may truly believe you.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Dɛ berɛ lolɛ onɔ la jiseswɛm aa vɔwɛ o nɔn la. Asɛ ba pa o sɔn Sɛra.
translated: At that time, Jesus was born and they named him Sarah.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Belaa haah la ta lawɛ o yal kpoŋe dɛ ŋɔ, “Gel n ne he chɔ.” O ta sɛɛh, dɛ yo lɛ o te dɛ chwa le buse, asɛ a ka o yal la do haah la nɔn ta.
translated: When Belaa was going, his friend told him, "Come, let's go and see." He refused, and said that he would go later in the evening, because he had his friend waiting for him at home.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: Asɛ o bɔɔ ba ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he so sum ne ɡɛ nɔɔ?”
translated: Then he called them and said, "Why are you doing this like this?

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Tɔpetooro wee la, kooro ɛɛ lee dɛ́ berɛ kaa he ne he tombena ta. Ásɛ he kpɔ kooro numol do o nɔn ta kɛŋɔ he fa aa dɛ doo sen la.
translated: When the sun rises, he will go to the farm with his hoe. He will find the work he has to do in the field.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Paanowɔɔra nomoa la aa noa kɛŋɔ Josef wono baɡle o dɔŋ la deera la mun tɛo a do som la, o bol o be ŋɔ, “N ɡe deero dɛ che paanoo wɛɡrɛre atoro.
translated: The chief priest came to meet Joseph's father and his brothers, who were sitting together. He said to them, "I have a dream that I want you to interpret.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: A habɔɔ tɔpetooro wee la, kooro la dɛ di o lola tɔpere, asɛ dɛ yerɛ o nomonome bwa. O le o sendoola nomoa la ne paanowɔɔra nomoa la, dɛ kpɔ ba ba o nɛra la sie.
translated: When the chief priest came, he would eat the food that was offered to him, and he would also drink the palm wine. He would take the kola nuts and the palm wine, and then he would bless the people.
